classification_rationale: This video is a medical case presentation/grand rounds lecture, not an FLS (Fundamentals of Laparoscopic Surgery) task performance. The content shows: (1) An auditorium setting with multiple attendees and a presenter at a podium; (2) Slide-based presentation with patient history, imaging (CT scans), and clinical case discussion; (3) Medical decision-making narrative about a urology patient with complications; (4) No laparoscopic surgical skill task performance (peg transfer, suturing, knot tying, pattern cutting, or endoloop deployment); (5) Presenter narration discussing diagnostic and clinical reasoning, not surgical technique instruction.

frame_analyses.content: Auditorium with presenter at podium and audience seated; TV monitor visible
Slide showing 'D.R.' patient history with HPI: colorectal cancer s/p APR, chemotherapy, ureteral stents
Slide showing family history and social history details
Clinical case question slide: 78-year-old male with colon cancer, presents with bleeding from GU tract
CT imaging of abdomen displayed on screen
Fluoroscopic imaging showing ureter with stent
Presenter visible at podium discussing case
Endoscopic imaging displayed showing ureter visualization
Another endoscopic imaging frame with similar ureter view
Slide showing 'Months Later' outcome: patient displeased with nephrostomy tube, attempted accommodation, antegrade stent placed
Presenter continuing presentation at podium
Additional CT imaging showing abdominal anatomy
Slide with case presentation text: 'My first day as a urology resident' scenario with bleeding and Foley bag findings
Follow-up case slide: 30-year-old male with ureteroarterial fistula post-endovascular stenting
Endoscopic/radiographic imaging of vascular structure
Presenter at podium with audience visible
Endoscopic imaging showing vascular anatomy with guidewire
Slide listing operative findings: right lower extremity embolectomy, femoral-femoral bypass, CFA ligation, etc.
Presenter continuing lecture at podium with seated audience
Slide showing case course: developed cold, pulseless leg on PPD3, occlusive embolus from distal right SFA

case_topics_covered: Ureteral stent complications post-colorectal cancer surgery
Vascular injury recognition and operative management
verbal_cues: Patient case history presentation with imaging review
teaching_content.educational_value: Case-based learning focused on urology complications and clinical reasoning; not laparoscopic surgical skills training

conclusion: This video does not contain an FLS task performance and therefore cannot be scored using FLS rubrics. It is an educational presentation suitable for case-based learning but falls outside the scope of Fundamentals of Laparoscopic Surgery assessment.
